note: Hand-entered one-off events (e.g. from posters/flyers spotted in the wild, or curated holiday programming). Each entry is merged into the dataset alongside live-source events. Use a stable id, real lat/lon, and ISO local startDateTime / endDateTime with the Pacific offset.

events.title: Lifetime Activities Summer Camp Open House
events.description: Free preview of Lifetime Activities' summer camps for ages 5-15. Carnival-style tennis and pickleball stations, an exclusive Play More - Save More camp promo, and raffle prizes (Sky Zone gift cards, Pokemon cards, a free camp). Limited space — register via the QR on-site. Parents must supervise; no drop-offs.

events.title: Hillsborough Memorial Day Parade & Festival
events.description: Annual parade kicks off at 10am, followed by a music festival and the observance ceremony at North School field at 11:30am.

events.title: Rohnert Park Memorial Day Remembrance Ceremony
events.description: Annual remembrance ceremony at the Rohnert Park Community Center to honor fallen service members.

events.title: USS Pampanito Memorial Day Lost Boat Ceremony
events.description: Annual ceremony aboard the WWII submarine USS Pampanito at Pier 45 honoring the 52 American submarines lost in World War II.

events.title: FoodieLand Night Market — Memorial Day Weekend
events.description: Outdoor food festival with 175 vendors from across the globe, plus family-friendly entertainment. Runs Friday through Sunday of the weekend.

events.title: FoodieLand Night Market — Memorial Day Weekend
events.description: Outdoor food festival with 175 vendors from across the globe, plus family-friendly entertainment.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 26: Qatar vs. Switzerland
events.description: Group B group-stage match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara — one of six matches the Bay Area hosts. Kickoff 12:00 PM PT.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 26: Austria vs. Jordan
events.description: Group J group-stage match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara. Kickoff 9:00 PM PT.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 26: Türkiye vs. Paraguay
events.description: Group D group-stage match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara. Kickoff 8:00 PM PT.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 26: Jordan vs. Algeria
events.description: Group J group-stage match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara. Kickoff 8:00 PM PT.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 26: Paraguay vs. Australia
events.description: Group D group-stage match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara. Kickoff 7:00 PM PT.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 26: Round of 32 at Levi's Stadium
events.description: Round of 32 knockout match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara — the Group D winner versus a third-place qualifier. Kickoff 5:00 PM PT.

events.title: World Cup Fútbol Watch Party — Walnut Creek
events.description: Free public FIFA World Cup 2026 watch party hosted by the Bay Area Host Committee at the Lesher Center for the Arts, with big-screen matches and family activities.

events.title: Fútbol Gameday LIVE at the Presidio
events.description: Free FIFA World Cup 2026 fan zone on the Presidio Main Parade Lawn with live match screenings, food, and games — an official Bay Area Host Committee celebration.

events.title: It's a Fútbol Thing — Great Mall
events.description: Free family World Cup 2026 fan event at the Great Mall in Milpitas with watch-party screens and soccer activities, part of the official Bay Area Host Committee program.

events.title: Brazil Fest — Menlo Park
events.description: Free World Cup 2026 celebration of Brazilian culture, music, and soccer in downtown Menlo Park, part of the official Bay Area Host Committee fan-zone program.

events.title: World Cup Watch Party: Mexico vs. South Korea
events.description: Free FIFA World Cup 2026 watch party at Mexican Heritage Plaza in San Jose for the Mexico vs. South Korea match, with food and community festivities.

events.title: Sunnyvale World Soccer & Food Fest
events.description: Free World Cup 2026 festival at Cityline Sunnyvale with global food vendors, live match screenings, and family soccer activities.

events.title: Taste of Milpitas — World Cup Edition
events.description: Free community food festival and World Cup 2026 watch party at the Milpitas Community Center with local restaurants and big-screen matches.

events.title: World Cup Watch Party: Mexico vs. Czechia
events.description: Free FIFA World Cup 2026 watch party at Mexican Heritage Plaza in San Jose for the Mexico vs. Czechia match, with food and community festivities.

events.title: SF Pride World Cup Block Party — USA vs. Türkiye
events.description: Free official SF Pride World Cup block party on Yerba Buena Lane for the USA vs. Türkiye match, with watch screens, DJs, and food.

events.title: San Mateo World Cup Fan Zone
events.description: Free FIFA World Cup 2026 fan zone at Central Park in San Mateo with a big screen and family activities, hosted by the Bay Area Host Committee.

events.title: Footvolley Exhibition — Santa Cruz Main Beach
events.description: Free World Cup 2026 footvolley exhibition on Santa Cruz Main Beach, part of the official Bay Area Host Committee beachfront program.

events.title: Beach Soccer Exhibition — Santa Cruz Main Beach
events.description: Free World Cup 2026 beach soccer exhibition match on Santa Cruz Main Beach, part of the official Bay Area Host Committee beachfront program.

events.title: Richmond World Cup Fan Zone — East Brother Beer Co.
events.description: Free FIFA World Cup 2026 watch party at East Brother Beer Company in Richmond with live matches on the big screen. 21+.

events.title: USA vs. Australia Watch Party — Oakland Marriott
events.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 watch party for the USA vs. Australia match at the Oakland Marriott City Center, with big screens and drinks. 21+.

events.title: North Beach Festival 2026
events.description: San Francisco's beloved North Beach street festival fills the historic Italian neighborhood with arts and crafts, live music, food, and hands-on family fun including Lego-building, bubble-making, a kids' chalk zone, and the annual Blessing of the Animals at 1pm. Held over Father's Day weekend; free to attend.
events.venue: North Beach (Washington Square Park & Grant Avenue)

events.title: North Beach Festival 2026
events.description: San Francisco's long-running North Beach street fair returns for Father's Day weekend, filling 11 blocks with 200+ vendors, live music stages, Italian street painting, art, and neighborhood food. Sunday includes the beloved Blessing of the Animals at 1 PM, making it a fun, free outing for the whole family.

events.title: San Francisco Juneteenth Parade & Block Party
events.description: San Francisco's fourth annual Juneteenth Parade marches down Market Street starting at 2nd Street, with music, performances, and community groups celebrating Black freedom, heritage, and joy, followed by a block party. Free and open to all ages.

events.title: San Francisco Dragon Boat Festival: Friendship Races
events.description: Watch dragon boat teams race across Lake Merced during this lively cultural festival celebrating Asian heritage. Families can enjoy the friendship races along with community activities on the southwest side of San Francisco. Presented in partnership with the Community Youth Center of San Francisco.

events.title: San Francisco Dragon Boat Festival: Friendship Races
events.description: Day two of the San Francisco Dragon Boat Festival's Friendship Races, with teams paddling colorful dragon boats across Lake Merced. Families can watch the races and enjoy cultural activities and vendors along the shore. A festive, free Father's Day weekend outing by the water.

events.title: Juneteenth Bayview Father's Day Festival 2026
events.description: A free family festival in the Bayview celebrating Juneteenth and Father's Day at Gilman Playground, with live music performances, a kids zone, and food and retail vendors. All ages welcome; RSVP recommended.

events.title: Golden Gate Park Band: Juneteenth & Father's Day Concert
events.description: The Golden Gate Park Band's free Sunday concert at the Spreckels Temple of Music bandshell, honoring Juneteenth and Father's Day with a joyful program of soul, rhythm, and American musical heritage. The band performs alongside vocalists Yvonne Cobbs, Robin Hodge-Williams, and Mary Williams from the Lorraine Hansberry Theatre's choir. Bring a blanket and the whole family to this outdoor afternoon of live music.

events.title: Black Family Reunion at the AAACC: Day 3
events.description: The AAACC's annual Black Family Reunion wraps its three-day run with live music, vendor booths, figure-drawing instruction, an Emory Douglas exhibit, and the SF Black Film Festival, all celebrating Black culture and liberation in San Francisco. A grab-and-go breakfast and family-friendly activities run throughout the day.

events.title: de Young Museum Free Saturday for Bay Area Residents
events.description: Every Saturday the de Young offers free general admission to residents of the nine Bay Area counties, with access to its permanent collection galleries in Golden Gate Park. Bring valid ID to redeem. The special exhibition Monet and Venice (ticketed) is on view through July 26.
events.cost: Free general admission for Bay Area residents (special exhibitions ticketed)

events.title: SF Zoo Celebrate Pride
events.description: The San Francisco Zoo celebrates Pride Month with a family-friendly event featuring lively music and a Drag Queen Storytime, set among the zoo's animal exhibits. Included with regular zoo admission.

events.title: 39th Annual Berkeley Juneteenth Festival
events.description: The 39th Annual Berkeley Juneteenth Festival fills Adeline Street in South Berkeley (steps from Ashby BART) with 100+ vendors, two live music stages, and a Kid Zone celebrating Black culture, history, food, and music. Free and family-friendly.

events.title: Oakland Ballers: Day of the Dad (Father's Day Game)
events.description: The Oakland Ballers host the Modesto Roadsters at Raimondi Park for a Father's Day afternoon ballgame with "Day of the Dad" family festivities. A fun, affordable outdoor outing for dads and kids.

events.title: Alameda County Fair - Opening Weekend
events.description: The Alameda County Fair (June 19-July 12, 2026) is open Saturday from noon to 11pm with carnival rides, a petting zoo, livestock, fair food, and live entertainment. A drone show lights up the sky at 9:30pm, capping a classic all-ages summer fair day in the Tri-Valley.

events.title: Father's Day at the Fair - Alameda County Fair
events.description: Celebrate Father's Day at the Alameda County Fair with a full day of family fun, including the Demolition Derby (Musical Barrels 1:30pm, Figure 8 Races 3:30pm, Finals 6pm), carnival rides, animal demonstrations, food vendors, and a drone show at 9:30pm. A big, high-energy outing for the whole family.

events.title: Open Cockpit Day (Father's Day)
events.description: Climb into the cockpits of historic aircraft including the P-51 Mustang, Harrier Jump Jet and MiG-15 at this Father's Day event, with live music, demonstrations and food service. Optional flight simulator and Short Solent Flying Boat tour available for an extra fee.

events.title: Oakland's Magic Kitchen Cooking Show
events.description: At Children's Fairyland's Aesop's Theatre, "Oakland's Magic Kitchen" is a live, kid-friendly cooking show where Fairyland teams up with a local chef (Adiam Tsegaye of Mela Bistro) and mascot Quercus to get little ones excited about ingredients, recipes, and making meals. Included with general admission; first-come, first-served seating.

events.title: Niles Canyon Railway Heritage Train Rides
events.description: Ride a vintage diesel heritage train on a scenic roundtrip through Niles Canyon, departing from the historic Sunol Depot. The excursion runs about an hour and twenty minutes, with departures at 10:30 AM and 1:00 PM, making a classic Father's Day weekend outing for the whole family.

events.title: Niles Canyon Railway Diesel Train Rides
events.description: Ride a vintage diesel train on a scenic 1 hour 20 minute roundtrip through Niles Canyon from the historic Sunol Station. Two departures (10:30am and 1:00pm) make this a classic, low-key family outing along a preserved stretch of the original transcontinental railroad route.

events.title: 5th Annual Jon Snyder Memorial Father's Day Car Show
events.description: Celebrate Father's Day at the Blackhawk Museum with a community car show featuring classic cars, custom builds, and fan-favorite film cars in the museum courtyard. Families enjoy live music from The Juke Joint Band, food trucks, a Lucid Motors display, and a 9-hole inflatable mini golf course for all ages, with all five museum galleries open and free admission for dads.
events.cost: Free for spectators; dads receive free museum admission. Car registration $50/one car

events.title: King of the County BBQ Challenge & Music Festival
events.description: A free outdoor festival at the Martinez waterfront featuring a backyard BBQ competition, live music, 75+ arts and craft vendor booths, food, and a dedicated Kids Zone with rides and activities. Family-friendly fun for all ages with free admission and free parking.

events.title: King of the County BBQ Challenge & Music Festival (Father's Day)
events.description: Celebrate Father's Day at the King of the County BBQ Challenge & Music Festival on the Martinez waterfront, featuring championship BBQ teams, live music, vendor booths, and a Kids Zone with rides and activities for all ages. Free admission and free parking.

events.title: Duarte Garage & Lincoln Highway Museum Open House
events.description: The Livermore Heritage Guild opens the historic 1915 Duarte Garage and Lincoln Highway Museum, sited on the original first transcontinental highway, for a family-friendly open house. Part of Livermore's 150th anniversary celebration, it offers a free look at vintage cars and local history on the third Sunday of the month.
events.neighborhood: North L Street at Portola Avenue

events.title: Opera in the Rose Garden
events.description: Opera San José presents a free outdoor concert at the historic Municipal Rose Garden, with singers and the Opera San José Orchestra performing beloved selections from Carmen, Rigoletto, Die Fledermaus, Pagliacci and The Pearl Fishers. Families of all ages are welcome to bring blankets, lawn chairs and picnics; local vendors will be on-site with complimentary Kona Ice for everyone. Doors and check-in open at 5:00pm; the concert begins at 6:00pm.

events.title: San Jose Giants vs. Lake Elsinore Storm (Father's Day Weekend Fireworks)
events.description: Catch the San Jose Giants take on the Lake Elsinore Storm at Excite Ballpark on a summer Saturday evening, part of a Father's Day weekend homestand with postgame fireworks. An affordable, classic outdoor outing the whole family can enjoy together.

events.title: San Jose Giants vs. Lake Elsinore Storm (Father's Day)
events.description: Catch a family-friendly afternoon of minor league baseball as the San Jose Giants host the Lake Elsinore Storm on Father's Day at Excite Ballpark, with first pitch at 1:00 pm. The Father's Day game caps the June 19-21 weekend and includes a Gigante Golf Driver Cover giveaway.

events.title: San Jose World Cup Soccer Celebration at San Pedro Square Market
events.description: San Jose's free World Cup Soccer Celebration turns the open-air San Pedro Square Market into a soccer party, with every tournament match shown live on jumbo screens, including a nearly 500-square-foot flagship screen, plus inflatables and family fan zones. A lively, free downtown destination for families during the World Cup.

events.title: San Jose World Cup Fan Festival at San Pedro Square Market
events.description: San Jose's free World Cup fan festival transforms the open-air San Pedro Square Market into a soccer celebration, showing matches live on a giant screen alongside fan zones, inflatables, face painting, youth activities, and local food. A lively, free downtown spot for families during the tournament.

events.title: Ube Festival 2026
events.description: A vibrant all-day celebration of Filipino culture at the Cow Palace, featuring ube-inspired food vendors, live music and DJs, cultural performances, and an artisan market. Kids 5 and under get in free, making it an easy family outing.

events.title: Kids Rock! featuring James Henry & Samba2Samba Kids
events.description: A free morning kids' concert at Courthouse Square in downtown Redwood City featuring James Henry and the high-energy Samba2Samba Kids. Part of the City's Summer Series, the Kids Rock! show is packed with music, dancing, and family fun.

events.title: 36th Annual Vallejo Juneteenth Festival & Parade
events.description: Vallejo's flagship Juneteenth celebration returns for its 36th year with a parade followed by a free waterfront festival featuring live music, food, vendors, and family activities. The festival runs 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. at the Barbara Kondylis Waterfront Green.

events.title: Tiburon Classic & Exotic Car Show (26th Anniversary)
events.description: A free, family-friendly showcase of classic and exotic automobiles along the Tiburon waterfront at Shoreline Park, with food, ice cream, and beverages available for purchase by San Francisco Bay. This year marks the 26th anniversary, with Cadillac as the featured marque.

events.title: Petaluma Fair (Father's Day)
events.description: The closing day of the Petaluma Fair falls on Father's Day, featuring carnival rides, livestock exhibits, fair food, games and live entertainment at the Petaluma Fairgrounds. One all-inclusive admission covers rides, concerts and exhibits, making it a classic small-town summer fair for the whole family.

events.title: Father's Day on the Rails
events.description: Celebrate Father's Day at the Western Railway Museum with scenic rides on historic electric interurban trains and vintage streetcars through the Solano countryside. Families can also explore the railway exhibits and take guided walking tours.

events.title: Rianda House 12th Annual Classic Car Show (Rally-4-Rianda)
events.description: A free, family-friendly Father's Day classic car show in downtown St. Helena benefiting the Rianda House senior activity center. Browse showcased classic cars and enjoy food trucks, barbecue, and live music.

events.title: West Point Inn Father's Day Pancake Breakfast
events.description: A beloved Marin Father's Day tradition: hike or bike up Mount Tamalpais to the historic West Point Inn for a pancake breakfast with sweeping San Francisco Bay views. It is a fundraiser for the nonprofit inn; note that all 2026 breakfast dates are sold out.

events.title: Lavender Bloom Fest at Bees N Blooms
events.description: Wander a fragrant lavender labyrinth in full bloom at this Sonoma County flower farm, with self-guided farm access, U-cut lavender, and a food truck on site. June 21 is a jazz day with the John Kalleen Jazz Group performing midday.
events.venue: Bees N Blooms Flower Farm & Lavender Labyrinth
events.cost: $20 (jazz day; kids 12 and under free with paying adult)

events.title: Six Flags Discovery Kingdom
events.description: Northern California's combined theme park and animal park is open this Sunday with roller coasters, family rides, animal attractions, and live shows. A popular all-day family outing in the North Bay, open 11 a.m. to 7 p.m.

events.title: Roaring Camp Father's Day BBQ & Steam Train
events.description: Celebrate Father's Day in the redwoods with a ride on the Redwood Forest Steam Train or Santa Cruz Beach Train, plus a chuckwagon BBQ lunch, live music, gold panning, bounce houses, and mini tractors. A multi-generational outing for the whole family.
events.cost: Steam train ticket + BBQ platter pricing (see official site)

events.title: Father's Day on the Wharf
events.description: Celebrate Father's Day at Monterey's historic Old Fisherman's Wharf with festive decorations and family photo spots, whale watching and marine excursions, candy and ice cream shops, and waterfront dining. Enter the 'Dad's Favorite Story' contest for a chance to win Wharf Dollars prizes.

events.title: Downtown Santa Cruz Makers Market
events.description: A free, open-air artisan market on the 1100 block of Pacific Avenue featuring 40+ local makers and artists selling handmade goods. Held the third Sunday of every month, it makes for an easy, stroller-friendly stroll through downtown Santa Cruz. Leashed dogs are welcome.

events.title: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk
events.description: California's classic free-admission seaside amusement park is in full summer swing, with the historic Giant Dipper coaster, beachfront rides, arcade games, and oceanfront fun for all ages. Open daily in summer, with Saturday hours of 11am to 10pm.
events.cost: Free admission; rides require tickets or an unlimited-rides wristband

events.title: Free Admission for Dads on Father's Day
events.description: Dads get free admission to the Peanuts-themed Charles M. Schulz Museum on Father's Day. Explore the galleries together and relive Dad's favorite Peanuts moments as a family.
events.cost: Free for dads; regular admission otherwise

events.title: Richmond Juneteenth Parade & Festival
events.description: Richmond's longtime Juneteenth tradition features a parade through the city followed by a festival at Nicholl Park with live music, dance, cultural displays, food, local vendors, and family-friendly activities. Free and open to all ages.

events.title: Hayward Juneteenth Freedom Celebration
events.description: Hayward's annual Juneteenth Freedom Celebration moves to a new home at Mount Eden High School with a Juneteenth history reading, live music, DJs, food and craft vendors, and a kids zone with children's activities. Free and open to the whole family.

events.title: Los Altos 7th Annual Juneteenth Community Festival
events.description: Justice Vanguard's free 7th annual Juneteenth Community Festival at Hillview Park brings music, dancing, art, performances, food, and local vendors together for an all-ages celebration of progress, joy, and community across the Bay.

events.title: East Palo Alto Juneteenth Celebration
events.description: The Peninsula's longtime Juneteenth celebration returns to Bell Street Park as part of East Palo Alto's multi-day Juneteenth Jubilee, with food, live music, cultural performances, and community vendors. A free, family-friendly festival for all ages. (Start and end times are estimated from the prior year's schedule.)

events.title: Petaluma Fair
events.description: The Petaluma Fair brings carnival rides, livestock and barn tours, fair food, live concerts and family entertainment to the Sonoma-Marin Fairgrounds. One admission ticket covers rides, concerts, exhibits and complimentary ice cream — a classic all-ages summer fair.
events.cost: Paid admission (one ticket includes rides, concerts, exhibits)

events.title: Peggy Sue's All American Car Show & Cruise
events.description: The 22nd Annual Peggy Sue's All American Car Show & Cruise displays hundreds of classic American cars, hot rods and customs at the Sonoma County Fairgrounds. Families can wander the Show & Shine, enjoy live music, and see the cars up close before the downtown cruise.
events.venue: Sonoma County Event Center at the Fairgrounds

events.title: San Jose Earthquakes Celebration of Soccer, presented by SJ26
events.description: Official Bay Area Host Committee / SJ26 fan zone transforming the open-air San Pedro Square Market and surrounding streets into a month-long soccer festival. Every FIFA World Cup 2026 match is broadcast live on multiple large screens. June 27 features World Cup group-stage finale matches. Includes a Family-Friendly Soccer Village with dedicated youth zones, face painting, soccer activations, interactive games, food vendors and giveaways.

events.title: San Jose Earthquakes Celebration of Soccer presented by SJ26
events.description: Official Bay Area Host Committee / SJ26 fan zone transforming the open-air San Pedro Square Market and surrounding streets into a month-long soccer festival. Every FIFA World Cup 2026 match is broadcast live on multiple large screens. June 28 features the start of the Round of 32 knockout stage. Includes a Family-Friendly Soccer Village with dedicated youth zones, face painting, soccer activations, interactive games, food vendors and giveaways.

events.title: Alameda County Fair
events.description: The annual Alameda County Fair runs June 19-July 12, 2026, open Wednesday through Sunday. June 27 is an open Saturday. Family attractions include carnival rides, the Big O Tires Summer Concert Series, action sports shows, All Alaskan Racing Pigs, a nightly drone show and fireworks. The fairgrounds is also a designated Bay Area Host Committee World Cup fan-zone host site.
events.cost: Teens & adults $19.80, kids 6-12 $16.50, ages 5 & under free; parking $15

events.title: Alameda County Fair
events.description: The annual Alameda County Fair runs June 19-July 12, 2026, open Wednesday through Sunday. June 28 is an open Sunday. Family attractions include carnival rides, the Big O Tires Summer Concert Series, action sports shows, All Alaskan Racing Pigs, a nightly drone show and fireworks. The fairgrounds is also a designated Bay Area Host Committee World Cup fan-zone host site.
events.cost: Teens & adults $19.80, kids 6-12 $16.50, ages 5 & under free; parking $15

events.title: Golden Gate Park Band Free Concert: Around the World in 90 Minutes
events.description: Free outdoor Golden Gate Park Band concert at the Spreckels Temple of Music. 'Around the World in 90 Minutes' hops continents from Gottschalk to The Beatles, and KDFC's Maggie Clennon Reberg narrates Prokofiev's 'Peter and the Wolf' (a family favorite). No tickets or reservations needed; bring a blanket or use the bench seating.

events.title: Gabby's Dollhouse Fairylandia Tour
events.description: DreamWorks Animation and KidsGardening bring the Gabby's Dollhouse Fairylandia Tour to the SF Botanical Garden. Includes a character meet-and-greet with Gabby in Fairylandia-themed attire plus garden-themed crafts (Kitty Fairy crown, terrarium) and a commemorative activity booklet for each child. RSVP required for the meet-and-greet.
events.cost: Garden admission required (free for SF residents, members, kids 4 & under); RSVP for meet-and-greet

events.title: Gabby's Dollhouse Fairylandia Tour
events.description: DreamWorks Animation and KidsGardening bring the Gabby's Dollhouse Fairylandia Tour to the SF Botanical Garden. Includes a character meet-and-greet with Gabby in Fairylandia-themed attire plus garden-themed crafts (Kitty Fairy crown, terrarium) and a commemorative activity booklet for each child. RSVP required for the meet-and-greet.
events.cost: Garden admission required (free for SF residents, members, kids 4 & under); timed-entry ticket for meet-and-greet

events.title: Feathered Friends Garden Adventure: Birdwatching and Nature Art for Families
events.description: Family birdwatching and nature-art program led by art educator and SF birder Nancy Palmer. Starts with birdwatching basics, heads into the garden to observe birds, then a creative art session with natural materials. Ticket price includes one child and accompanying adult.
events.venue: San Francisco Botanical Garden (meet at Main Gate)
events.cost: $50 per ticket (covers one child plus accompanying parent/guardian)

events.title: Tiny Chef, Big Impact: The Show (Planetarium)
events.description: New 20-minute planetarium show starring internet sensation Tiny Chef, part of the limited-run 'Tiny Chef, Big Impact' takeover (extended through September 2026) that includes an exhibit, photo op, and Academy-wide activations. Show times on Sat Jun 27: 11:15 a.m. and 12:45 p.m.
events.venue: California Academy of Sciences, Morrison Planetarium

events.title: Tiny Chef, Big Impact: The Show (Planetarium)
events.description: New 20-minute planetarium show starring internet sensation Tiny Chef, part of the limited-run 'Tiny Chef, Big Impact' takeover (extended through September 2026) that includes an exhibit, photo op, and Academy-wide activations. Show times on Sun Jun 28: 11:15 a.m. and 12:45 p.m.
events.venue: California Academy of Sciences, Morrison Planetarium

events.title: Saturday Science: Drop-In Science Workshop for Families
events.description: Drop-in hands-on science workshop for families at the Randall Museum's Corona Heights location, offered Saturday mornings.
events.cost: $10 per adult/child combo; $5 per additional person

events.title: Meet an Animal Ambassador
events.description: Up-close encounter with one of the Randall Museum's live animal ambassadors, offered Saturday at the museum's Corona Heights location.

events.title: Afternoon Art: Saturday Drop-In Art Workshop for Families
events.description: Drop-in hands-on art workshop for families in the Randall Museum Art Room, with instructors guiding creative activities. Offered Saturday afternoons.

events.title: Creative Youth Celebration 2026
events.description: Free, public exhibition and performance event spotlighting the next generation of Bay Area creatives. Explore award-winning student art installations, film screenings, pop-up performances, and hands-on arts activities. Arts panel 1:00-1:45pm; keynote (Ruby Ibarra) and awards ceremony 3:00-4:00pm.
events.venue: Yerba Buena Center for the Arts (YBCA)
events.cost: Free (suggested $20 donation; no one turned away)

events.title: Gumbo Social's 4th Annual Bayview Block Party
events.description: Free, family-friendly block party celebrating Gumbo Social's 3rd anniversary in the Bayview. Features live music performances, local food and merch vendors, and special guests, bringing together local businesses, artists, musicians, and community members. Optional Food Tour tickets ($25) available.
events.venue: Gumbo Social (parking lot at 3rd St & Thornton Ave)

events.title: The Whale's Tail Beer/Wine Garden + Live Music
events.description: Free, family- and dog-friendly seasonal outdoor garden in Golden Gate Park with live music, lawn games, public art, and rotating food trucks. June 27 lineup features Jamie Wilson, Joe Burke, Paris, and Shimmy. Drinks (with N/A options) available for purchase.
events.venue: The Whale's Tail (14th Ave East Meadow, JFK Promenade)

events.title: The Whale's Tail Beer/Wine Garden + Live Music
events.description: Free, family- and dog-friendly seasonal outdoor garden in Golden Gate Park with live music, lawn games, public art, and rotating food trucks. June 28 lineup features Ants Fujinaga, Deborah Crooks, John Elliott, and Middle Dog. Drinks (with N/A options) available for purchase.
events.venue: The Whale's Tail (14th Ave East Meadow, JFK Promenade)

events.title: Pier Party at the Wharf
events.description: Free outdoor waterfront concert/party at Fisherman's Wharf with live music, local performers, dancing, and bistro-style seating. June 27 lineup: DJ Umami, Drag on Deck with Bobby Friday, and Analog Dog. Note: includes a craft-cocktail bar and drag programming, so the vibe is adult-leaning though the space is a free public waterfront area open to all.

events.title: LakeFest Oakland
events.description: Annual Oakland music and culture festival at Lake Merritt with a Main Stage, Culture Stage, Gospel Circle, a Health & Wellness Zone with farmers market, a Technology Zone, and a dedicated Kids Zone. The festival draws road closures around Lake Merritt this day (Children's Fairyland confirms it is closed June 27 due to LakeFest).
events.venue: Lake Merritt Sailboat House / Boathouse (Lakeside Park)
events.cost: $23.18 general admission (incl. fees); Kids Zone included

events.title: Five Dollar Day: Trapeze Arts (Les Aerielles)
events.description: Discounted Five Dollar Day at the Lawrence Hall of Science featuring annual trapeze arts shows by returning favorites Les Aerielles. Trapeze performances at 12:00 noon, 1:00 p.m., and 2:00 p.m.; $5 admission also covers the rest of the science center's exhibits. A free shuttle runs from Downtown Berkeley BART.
events.cost: $5 discounted admission per person (regular admission is $27 for age 3+)

events.title: Makers Market Outdoor Market at Broadway Plaza
events.description: Outdoor makers market and local artist street fair at Broadway Plaza in downtown Walnut Creek, featuring local makers and vendors.

events.title: Golden State Model Railroad Museum - Sunday Open Running
events.description: Operating-trains Sunday at the 10,000-square-foot Golden State Model Railroad Museum in Point Richmond, where multiple-scale model trains run on extensive layouts. A family-favorite marquee attraction open Sundays April-September.

events.title: 43rd Annual Fountain Blues & Brews Festival
events.description: Beloved Bay Area summer blues tradition returning for its 43rd year with live music, craft brews, food, artisan vendors, an Instrument Petting Zoo, and family-friendly fun.

events.title: 43rd Annual Fountain Blues & Brews Festival
events.description: Beloved Bay Area summer blues tradition returning for its 43rd year with live music, craft brews, food, artisan vendors, an Instrument Petting Zoo, and family-friendly fun.

events.title: Movie Nights in the Park: Selena (Community Block Party)
events.description: Free outdoor movie under the stars featuring a screening of 'Selena,' preceded by a community block party with a live DJ dance party, lowriders, and games starting at 6PM.

events.title: Cars & Croissants on The Row
events.description: Free morning gathering of supercars, luxury vehicles, and exotic marques on Santana Row, with coffee, croissants, and a vibrant family-friendly atmosphere.

events.title: FoodieLand Night Market - San Jose
events.description: Large family-friendly outdoor food festival inspired by Asian night markets, with 175+ multicultural food vendors across two food stages, carnival games, live music on two stages, and local artisan vendors. Saturday session of the three-day San Jose run.
events.cost: $12 general admission; children 5 and under free; parking $15/vehicle

events.title: FoodieLand Night Market - San Jose
events.description: Large family-friendly outdoor food festival inspired by Asian night markets, with 175+ multicultural food vendors, carnival games, live music on two stages, and local artisan vendors. Closing Sunday session of the three-day San Jose run.
events.cost: $12 general admission; children 5 and under free; parking $15/vehicle

events.title: Around the World Festival at Cityline Sunnyvale
events.description: Free downtown cultural festival in Cityline's Redwood Square featuring global food, live music and performers, local makers, art, a community dance activity, and a Kids' Creativity Station (button-making with Busy Playbox).

events.title: Happy Birds
events.description: Live performing-birds show featuring an elite team of parrots, macaws and cockatoos with wild and trained behaviors including impersonations, opera singing and more. A daytime special-event show at the Hiller Aviation Museum.
events.cost: Included with museum admission; Museum Members free

events.title: Captain Achoo's Big Pirate Adventure (Children's Redwood Theater)
events.description: Rabbit Hole Theater presents an immersive daytime outdoor kids' show in Filoli's redwood grove (Children's Redwood Theater), with silly antics, wild characters and pirate-sized fun. Bring a blanket. Designed for younger audiences.
events.cost: Free with General Admission Tickets; reservations required

events.title: Coastside Farmers' Market
events.description: Weekly Saturday Coastside Farmers' Market in Half Moon Bay with local produce, food and vendors at Shoreline Station. Family-friendly outdoor market.

events.title: Toyota/Save Mart 350 NASCAR Weekend: Pit Boss/FoodMaxx 250
events.description: NASCAR O'Reilly Auto Parts (Xfinity) Series Pit Boss/FoodMaxx 250 race at Sonoma Raceway, part of the Toyota/Save Mart 350 NASCAR Cup Series weekend in wine country. Spectator gates open at 10 a.m. All-ages motorsports event (loud; ear protection recommended for kids).

events.title: Toyota/Save Mart 350 NASCAR Cup Series
events.description: The 36th running of the Toyota/Save Mart 350 NASCAR Cup Series at Sonoma Raceway, Northern California's premier motorsports event. Spectator gates open at 7 a.m. All-ages event with kids tickets; loud, ear protection recommended.

events.title: Bubble Bash Summer Celebration
events.description: Bubble Bash is the Bay Area Discovery Museum's limited-time summer celebration featuring giant bubbles and joyful foam parties, included with museum admission. Geared to young children at this hands-on children's museum below the Golden Gate Bridge.

events.title: Bubble Bash Summer Celebration
events.description: Bubble Bash is the Bay Area Discovery Museum's limited-time summer celebration featuring giant bubbles and joyful foam parties, included with museum admission. Geared to young children at this hands-on children's museum below the Golden Gate Bridge.
events.cost: Included with museum admission ($25 kids & adults, $23 seniors, free under 12 months)

events.title: Hot Amphitheater Nights feat. Maya Latin Tribute Band
events.description: Free outdoor community summer concert in Novato's Hot Amphitheater Nights series, featuring the Maya Latin Tribute Band. Bring picnic blankets and lawn chairs; food available for purchase from local vendors. Pets asked to stay home.

events.title: Classic Car Show (Corks, Chrome, & Classics)
events.description: Free public classic car show at Charles Krug Winery in St. Helena featuring vintage and classic automobiles, on-site food vendors, and Napa Valley wines (wine for adults; winery has an age-gate). Marketed as a day for friends and family.

events.title: Pleasure Point Street Fair
events.description: Annual neighborhood street fair along Portola Drive in the Pleasure Point surf district, with live music, local vendors, food, and a dedicated kids' zone featuring crafts, face painting, and henna.
events.venue: Portola Drive (between 38th and 41st Avenues), Pleasure Point

events.title: Woodies on the Wharf (30th Annual Car Show)
events.description: The 30th annual Woodies on the Wharf displays over 200 pre-1950s wooden-bodied 'woodie' station wagons along the Santa Cruz Municipal Wharf, ending with a send-off parade. Free to attend with shops, restaurants, and coastal views.
events.venue: Santa Cruz Municipal Wharf, 21 Municipal Wharf

events.title: Sophia's Sunday Morning West Cliff Drive Woodie Cruise
events.description: Closing event of the Woodies on the Wharf weekend: a Sunday-morning cruise of vintage woodie wagons along scenic West Cliff Drive, returning to the Santa Cruz Harbor for coffee and pastries. Free to watch.
events.venue: Santa Cruz Harbor (cruise along West Cliff Drive)

events.title: Pinocchio — Children's Musical Theater San Jose
events.description: Children's Musical Theater San Jose presents Pinocchio, the classic tale of a wooden puppet who wants to be a real boy, brought to life with inventive puppetry and a cast of local performers ages 7-10. Saturday performances at 2pm and 7pm.

events.title: Pinocchio – CMT Junior Talents
events.description: Children's Musical Theater San Jose presents Pinocchio, the classic tale of a wooden puppet who wants to be a real boy, brought to life with inventive puppetry and a cast of local performers ages 7-10. Sunday performances at 11am and 3pm; closing day of the run.

events.title: Denève Conducts Saint-Saëns' Organ Symphony — San Francisco Symphony
events.description: Stéphane Denève conducts the San Francisco Symphony with organist Olivier Latry in a program of Connesson's Flammenschrift, Poulenc's Organ Concerto, and Saint-Saëns' Symphony No. 3 'Organ.' A grand all-ages orchestral concert (note: a standard subscription concert, not a dedicated children's program).

events.title: Chamber Music at Davies — San Francisco Symphony Musicians
events.description: San Francisco Symphony musicians present a chamber music concert at Davies Symphony Hall, with works by Gareth Farr, Sarn Oliver, Joan Tower, and Fauré. An all-ages classical concert (note: chamber recital, not a dedicated children's program).

events.title: "The Magic Tea Kettle" Puppet Show at Children's Fairyland
events.description: Children's Fairyland's historic Storybook Puppet Theater (the oldest continuously operating puppet theater in the U.S.) presents puppet shows three times daily at 11am, 2pm, and 4pm. Note: Fairyland is CLOSED Saturday June 27 due to LakeFest road closures and reopens Sunday June 28.

events.title: Evening at the Farm (After-Hours Naturalist Walk)
events.description: A special after-hours, ranger-led twilight walk at Ardenwood Historic Farm. Join a Naturalist for a peaceful walk as day fades to night and discover how dusk changes the farm's animals, the sky, and the mood. Registration required; participants must be 7+ and accompanied by an adult.
events.cost: Park entry (Saturday): $4/adult (18+), $2/child (4-17), free under 4; registration required

events.title: Saturday Farm Programs at Ardenwood Historic Farm
events.description: A day of hands-on living-history programs at this working historic farm in Fremont. Saturday June 27 schedule: Meet the Chickens (10:30-11am), Corn Mosaics craft using freshly harvested Indian corn (11:30am-12:30pm), and the daily Animal Feeding at the Granary (3pm). Costumed staff, narrow-gauge train rides, and the Patterson House Victorian farmhouse round out the day.

events.title: Sunday Farm Programs at Ardenwood Historic Farm
events.description: Hands-on living-history programs at this working historic farm. Sunday June 28 schedule: Wake Up the Farm morning animal chores (10:30-11am), Amazing Maize woodstove corn treat & history (11:30am-12:30pm), Hay, Hay, Hay! 1850s-style hay harvest wagon program (1:30-2:30pm), and the daily Animal Feeding (3pm). Narrow-gauge train rides and Patterson House tours also operate.

events.title: Circus Bella 'AH HA!' Free Family Circus at Yerba Buena Gardens 2026
events.description: Bring the kids for a free, all-ages outdoor circus as Circus Bella premieres 'AH HA!' on the Great Lawn at Yerba Buena Gardens. Two free Saturday shows on June 27 (12:00pm and 2:15pm) feature acrobats, jugglers, and live music for the whole family.

events.title: Free LA Children's Chorus & SF Boys Chorus Concert (July 3, 2026)
events.description: Enjoy a free, all-ages evening of choral music as the Grammy Award-winning Los Angeles Children's Chorus joins the San Francisco Boys Chorus for a joint concert at Calvary Presbyterian Church on Friday, July 3, 2026. Families are welcome to this kid-friendly performance in Pacific Heights to kick off the July 4th weekend.

events.title: San Francisco 4th of July Waterfront Fireworks 2026 (Pier 39 / Fisherman's Wharf)
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day with San Francisco's free July 4th fireworks show, launched from barges on the Bay near PIER 39 and Aquatic Park beginning around 9:30pm. Bring the family early for free live music along Fisherman's Wharf, then find a spot on the waterfront to watch the roughly 20-minute display light up the sky.

events.title: Fillmore Jazz Festival 2026 - Free SF Street Festival (Saturday, July 4)
events.description: The Fillmore Jazz Festival returns July 4-5, 2026, as one of San Francisco's largest free outdoor music festivals. Stroll Fillmore Street between Eddy and Jackson for live jazz on multiple stages, arts and crafts vendors, and family-friendly food in the historic Fillmore District, 10am to 6pm.

events.title: Free Family Ukulele Jam at Yerba Buena Gardens (July 4, 2026)
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July with free, all-ages music as SF Uke Jam presents 'Summer Uke-splosion: Peace, Love and Ukulele' at Yerba Buena Gardens. This free outdoor Yerba Buena Gardens Festival event runs from 12:00pm to 4:00pm in the heart of downtown San Francisco.

events.title: Fillmore Jazz Festival 2026 - Free SF Street Festival (Sunday, July 5)
events.description: Day two of the free Fillmore Jazz Festival continues Sunday, July 5, 2026, along Fillmore Street between Eddy and Jackson. Enjoy live jazz on multiple stages, hundreds of arts and crafts vendors, and family-friendly food in San Francisco's historic Fillmore District, 10am to 6pm.

events.title: Redwood City 4th of July Parade 2026
events.description: Northern California's largest Independence Day parade winds 1.3 miles through historic downtown Redwood City starting at 10 a.m. This free, family-friendly July 4th tradition features marching bands, floats, and community groups celebrating America's 250th birthday. Arrive early for a great curbside spot along Broadway.

events.title: Redwood City Fourth of July Fireworks 2026 at the Port
events.description: Cap off Independence Day with a patriotic fireworks show over the Port of Redwood City, starting around 9:30 p.m. on July 4, 2026. This free July 4th fireworks display is a Peninsula favorite for families.
events.venue: Port of Redwood City (675 Seaport Blvd)

events.title: Half Moon Bay Ol' Fashioned 4th of July Parade 2026
events.description: Half Moon Bay's beloved Ol' Fashioned 4th of July Parade marches down Main Street at 12 noon on July 4, 2026. This free, small-town Coastside tradition brings out floats, classic cars, and the whole community for a family-friendly Independence Day. The parade lasts about 90 minutes.

events.title: Half Moon Bay 4th of July Pancake Breakfast 2026
events.description: Start Independence Day on the Coastside with the Half Moon Bay Lions Club pancake breakfast on Main Street, served 8:00 to 11:30 a.m. on July 4, 2026. A hearty, family-friendly kickoff before the noon Ol' Fashioned Parade.
events.venue: Main Street, Half Moon Bay (Half Moon Bay Lions Club)

events.title: Pacifica 4th of July Celebration 2026 at Frontierland Park
events.description: Pacifica's free Independence Day celebration takes over Frontierland Park from 11 a.m. to 2 p.m. on July 4, 2026. Families can enjoy games, rides, live music, entertainment, and community vendors at this all-ages coastal July 4th party.

events.title: San Mateo 4th of July in the Park 2026 at Central Park
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day at San Mateo's Central Park from 11 a.m. to 2 p.m. on July 4, 2026. This free, family-friendly event features kids' crafts and games, a flag-raising and national anthem at 11:45 a.m., and a live concert beginning at noon.

events.title: Foster City 4th of July Celebration 2026 at Leo Ryan Park
events.description: Foster City's free Fourth of July celebration returns to Leo J. Ryan Memorial Park on July 4, 2026 with a Rotary Club pancake breakfast, live music, and the popular Family & Dog Parade. Daytime festivities run through 4 p.m. Note: there is no fireworks show in 2026 due to community center construction.

events.title: Redwood City Kids Rock 2026 Free Family Concert at Courthouse Square
events.description: Kids Rock! brings a free Saturday-morning kids' music party to Redwood City's Courthouse Square on June 27, 2026, from 10 a.m. to noon. Hosted by MC Andy-Z with music, dancing, and an inflatable playland, it's a fun, family-friendly outing for little ones.

events.title: Great America Star-Spangled Nights Fireworks 2026, Santa Clara (July 3)
events.description: Kick off the Fourth of July weekend at California's Great America in Santa Clara with Star-Spangled Nights, featuring patriotic music, family rides and a spectacular fireworks finale at 9:40 PM. A festive, all-ages way to start the July 4th holiday in Silicon Valley.

events.title: Great America Star-Spangled Nights Fireworks 2026, Santa Clara (July 4)
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July at California's Great America in Santa Clara with Star-Spangled Nights, bringing patriotic music, family rides and a big fireworks show at 9:40 PM. A classic all-ages July 4th celebration for families in the South Bay.

events.title: Milpitas Red, White & Boom 2026 Fireworks & Concert (July 4)
events.description: Milpitas' signature Fourth of July celebration, Red, White & Boom, returns to the Milpitas Sports Center with live music, family activities and a big fireworks show after dark. Gates open at 5 PM for a fun, all-ages July 4th party in the South Bay.
events.cost: Presale $8, general admission $11, ages 2 & under $3 (plus fees)

events.title: Morgan Hill Freedom Fest Independence Day Parade 2026 (July 4)
events.description: One of Northern California's biggest hometown Fourth of July parades, the Morgan Hill Freedom Fest parade kicks off at 10:00 AM through downtown Morgan Hill. A free, family-friendly July 4th tradition with floats, marching bands and community pride.

events.title: Morgan Hill Freedom Fest Fireworks on the Green 2026 (July 4)
events.description: Cap off Independence Day at Morgan Hill's Fireworks on the Green, one of the largest fireworks shows in Northern California, at the Morgan Hill Outdoor Sports Center. Gates open at 3 PM and admission is free for the whole family on the Fourth of July.

events.title: Mountain View 4th of July Fireworks Spectacular with San Francisco Symphony 2026
events.description: Mountain View's Fourth of July Fireworks Spectacular returns to Shoreline Amphitheatre with the San Francisco Symphony and a grand fireworks finale. A memorable, all-ages way to celebrate July 4th under the stars in the South Bay.

events.title: Alameda 4th of July Parade 2026 (Longest in the Nation)
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day at Alameda's famous Fourth of July Parade, one of the longest in the nation, stepping off at 10 a.m. from Park Street and Lincoln Avenue. Families line the 3.3-mile route for marching bands, floats, antique cars, equestrians and community groups. Free to watch.
events.venue: Park Street (start: Park St & Lincoln Ave)

events.title: Alameda Mayor's July 4th Fun Run & R.A.C.E. 2026
events.description: Kick off the Fourth of July in Alameda with the Mayor's July 4th Fun Run, a family-friendly 5K that steps off at 8:30 a.m. on Park Street, right before the famous Independence Day parade. Walk or run with kids and neighbors to benefit the Midway Shelter of Alameda.

events.title: Piedmont 4th of July Parade & Picnic 2026
events.description: Piedmont's classic small-town Fourth of July Parade marches down Highland Avenue at 11 a.m. on Saturday, July 4, 2026, followed by a free Picnic in the Park with live music in Piedmont Park until 3 p.m. A beloved family-friendly Independence Day tradition for all ages.

events.title: El Cerrito worldOne 4th of July Festival 2026 (Free)
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July in El Cerrito at the free City of El Cerrito & worldOne Festival in Cerrito Vista Park, 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. This multicultural family festival features world-class music, a classic car showcase, kids' rides, bounce houses and food booths. Free admission with pay-as-you-go attractions.

events.title: San Leandro 4th of July Celebration 2026 (Free Family Festival)
events.description: San Leandro marks America's 250th with a free Fourth of July community celebration at the waterfront Monarch Bay Golf Club, 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. Families enjoy live entertainment, a vendor village with food and crafts, and a Kids Zone packed with games, inflatables and hands-on activities for all ages.
events.venue: Monarch Bay Golf Club (13800 Monarch Bay Dr)

events.title: Danville Kiwanis 4th of July Parade 2026
events.description: One of the Bay Area's largest Independence Day celebrations returns for its 51st year. Families line Hartz Avenue for the free Danville Kiwanis July 4th Parade with marching bands, floats, scouts, and classic cars. Arrive early for a great curbside spot.
events.venue: Downtown Danville (Hartz Avenue & San Ramon Valley Boulevard)

events.title: Concord 4th of July Parade 2026 at Todos Santos Plaza
events.description: Concord's free Independence Day Parade marches through historic downtown around Todos Santos Plaza, kicking off a full day of all-ages July 4th festivities. The morning also features a Stars & Stripes kids fun run, 5K, and pancake breakfast.

events.title: Concord 4th of July Fireworks 2026 at Mt. Diablo High School
events.description: Cap off the Fourth of July with Concord's big fireworks show over Mt. Diablo High School. The afternoon festival opens at 4 p.m. with live music, food, and carnival fun before the fireworks light up the sky at 9 p.m.
events.venue: Mt. Diablo High School, 2450 Grant St
events.cost: Festival admission; fireworks show free to view from surrounding area

events.title: Martinez 4th of July Parade 2026 Downtown Main Street
events.description: Celebrate America's 250th and Martinez's 150th anniversary at the free Independence Day Parade down historic Main Street. Families enjoy marching units, civic groups, classic vehicles, and community floats in downtown Martinez.
events.venue: Downtown Martinez Main Street (Court St & Main St)

events.title: Fremont 4th of July Parade 2026 Downtown
events.description: Fremont's free Fourth of July Parade rolls down Paseo Padre Parkway in downtown, celebrating America's 250th and Fremont's 70th anniversary. Bring the family for floats, bands, and a community after-party at the Downtown Event Center.

events.title: Alameda County Fair July 4th Fireworks Spectacular 2026 in Pleasanton
events.description: The Alameda County Fair closes out July 4th with a massive fireworks show honoring America's 250th anniversary. Spend the day on carnival rides and at fair attractions, then watch the red, white, and blue grand finale light up the Pleasanton sky.

events.title: Alameda County Fair 2026 in Pleasanton (Friday July 3)
events.description: Start the July 4th weekend at the Alameda County Fair in Pleasanton. Families can ride the carnival, sample fair food, visit the livestock barns, and catch Arena Cross Nationals motorcycle racing.

events.title: Alameda County Fair 2026 in Pleasanton (Sunday July 5)
events.description: Wrap up the July 4th weekend with a family day at the Alameda County Fair in Pleasanton. Enjoy carnival rides, fair food, animal exhibits, and Arena Cross Nationals racing before the fair's final week.

events.title: Marin County Fair 2026 with Nightly Fireworks - San Rafael (July 3)
events.description: Celebrate the 2026 Marin County Fair in San Rafael with five days of carnival rides, live music, exhibits and family fun under the theme 'Stars, Stripes & Stories.' Admission includes spectacular fireworks over the lagoon every night to kick off the July 4th holiday weekend. A classic all-ages tradition at the Marin Center fairgrounds.
events.cost: Admission ticket required (includes rides, concerts & fireworks)

events.title: Marin County Fair 2026: July 4th Fireworks & Carnival - San Rafael
events.description: Spend the Fourth of July at the 2026 Marin County Fair in San Rafael, packed with carnival rides, live music, exhibits and family-friendly fun. Admission includes free July 4th fireworks over the lagoon, making this a marquee all-ages Independence Day destination in Marin.
events.cost: Admission ticket required (includes rides, concerts & fireworks)

events.title: Marin County Fair 2026 Final Night Fireworks - San Rafael (July 5)
events.description: Catch the closing day of the 2026 Marin County Fair in San Rafael with carnival rides, live music, exhibits and one last night of fireworks over the lagoon. A fun, all-ages way to wrap up July 4th weekend at the Marin Center fairgrounds.
events.cost: Admission ticket required (includes rides, concerts & fireworks)

events.title: Novato 4th of July Parade 2026 & Buckaroo Pancake Breakfast
events.description: The free Novato 4th of July Parade 2026 marches down Grant Avenue with more than one hundred entries, making it one of the largest Independence Day parades in the Bay Area. Arrive early for the Buckaroo Pancake Breakfast (7-10 a.m.) and free pre-parade entertainment starting at 9 a.m. A beloved family-friendly July 4th tradition celebrating America's 250th birthday.

events.title: Sonoma Hometown 4th of July Parade & Fireworks 2026 - Sonoma Plaza
events.description: Sonoma's Hometown 4th of July Parade & Fireworks 2026 fills the historic Sonoma Plaza with an old-fashioned parade, live music, food vendors and a kid-friendly splash zone, capped by a 50+ year fireworks tradition at dusk. This free, family-friendly celebration has been named one of the best July 4th events in the country.

events.title: Healdsburg Fourth of July Kids' Parade & Duck Dash 2026 - Healdsburg Plaza
events.description: Healdsburg's free Fourth of July Kids' Parade and Duck Dash 2026 turns the Healdsburg Plaza into a family party with a kids' parade (sign-up 10 a.m., step-off 11 a.m.), Duck Dash races, live music, lawn games and face painting. A festive, all-ages July 4th tradition in downtown Healdsburg wine country.

events.title: Healdsburg Independence Day Fireworks 2026 - July 4th Show
events.description: Healdsburg's Independence Day Fireworks 2026 light up the sky at 9:30 p.m. on July 4th, launching from the high school area with free viewing at the high school parking lot and points throughout town. A dazzling, family-friendly way to cap off the Fourth of July in Sonoma County.

events.title: Petaluma 4th of July Fireworks Show 2026 - America's 250th
events.description: Petaluma's 4th of July Fireworks Show 2026 returns with a high-elevation display launching from the Petaluma Fairgrounds at 9:30 p.m., easily viewed for free from parks and neighborhoods across town. Celebrate America's 250th birthday with this dazzling, family-friendly fireworks tradition.

events.title: Aptos World's Shortest Parade 2026 - July 4th Family Celebration
events.description: Aptos hosts its famous World's Shortest Parade, a beloved free Fourth of July tradition for the whole family. Watch decorated floats, marching groups, and costumes wind a half-mile down Soquel Drive, then stay for the 4th of July Party in the Park. A classic all-ages July 4th celebration on the Santa Cruz County coast.

events.title: Spirit of Watsonville 4th of July Parade 2026 - Free Downtown Celebration
events.description: The Spirit of Watsonville 4th of July Parade brings floats, marching bands, and community groups down Main Street in Historic Downtown Watsonville. This free, all-ages Independence Day parade is a Pajaro Valley tradition the whole family can enjoy.

events.title: Scotts Valley 4th of July Parade & Flyover 2026 - Free Family Festival
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July in Scotts Valley with a free family festival featuring a parade, a flyover, a kids zone with free activities, and food trucks. The parade and flyover step off at 11:00 am along Scotts Valley Drive, with festivities running through the afternoon.
events.venue: Scotts Valley Drive (Boys & Girls Clubs of Santa Cruz County)

events.title: 4th of July on the Wharf 2026 - Old Fisherman's Wharf Monterey
events.description: Spend the Fourth of July at Monterey's Old Fisherman's Wharf, decked out in festive red, white, and blue decor for fun family photo ops. Stroll the wharf, shop, dine, watch for whales, and soak up the holiday on Monterey Bay. A free, family-friendly way to celebrate July 4th on the coast.

events.title: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk Evenings on the Colonnade 2026 - Free DJ Dance Party (Sat Jun 27)
events.description: Wrap up a summer Saturday at the Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk with a free DJ Dance Party as part of Evenings on the Colonnade. Families can show off their best moves near Neptune's Kingbeach. A free, all-ages way to enjoy live entertainment by the water.
events.venue: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk (Colonnade by Neptune's Kingdom)

events.title: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk Evenings on the Colonnade 2026 - Free DJ Dance Party (Sun Jun 28)
events.description: Cap off your weekend at the Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk with a free DJ Dance Party during Evenings on the Colonnade. This free, family-friendly summer series brings music and dancing to the beach near Neptune's Kingdom.
events.venue: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk (Colonnade by Neptune's Kingdom)

events.title: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk Evenings on the Colonnade 2026 - Free July 4th DJ Dance Party
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July at the Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk with a free DJ Dance Party during Evenings on the Colonnade. It's a fun, free, all-ages way to keep the holiday going by the beach near Neptune's Kingdom.
events.venue: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk (Colonnade by Neptune's Kingdom)

events.title: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk Evenings on the Colonnade 2026 - Free DJ Dance Party (Sun Jul 5)
events.description: Round out the July 4th weekend with a free DJ Dance Party at the Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk's Evenings on the Colonnade. Families can dance the evening away near Neptune's Kingdom in this free, all-ages beachside summer series.
events.venue: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk (Colonnade by Neptune's Kingdom)

events.title: Six Flags Discovery Kingdom July 4th Fest 2026 Drone & Fireworks (Vallejo)
events.description: Six Flags Discovery Kingdom in Vallejo rings in the Fourth of July weekend with Star-Spangled Nights, a dazzling evening drone and fireworks spectacular choreographed to patriotic music. Enjoy a full day of rides, animals, and family fun before the nighttime show lights up the sky. A perfect all-ages July 4th celebration in the Bay Area.

events.title: Six Flags Discovery Kingdom July 4th Fest 2026 Drone & Fireworks (Vallejo)
events.description: Six Flags Discovery Kingdom in Vallejo celebrates the Fourth of July with Star-Spangled Nights, a dazzling evening drone and fireworks spectacular choreographed to patriotic music. Enjoy a full day of rides, animals, and family fun before the nighttime show lights up the sky. A perfect all-ages July 4th celebration in the Bay Area.

events.title: Gilroy Gardens Cherry Red, White & Blue July 4th Weekend 2026
events.description: Gilroy Gardens Family Theme Park celebrates the Fourth of July weekend with the Cherry Red, White & Blue Celebration, July 3-5, 2026, featuring extended hours from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. Families can enjoy gentle rides, water play areas, a backyard BBQ, and the final weekend of the Cherry Jubilee. A wonderful all-ages July 4th outing in the South Bay.

events.title: Gilroy Gardens Cherry Red, White & Blue July 4th Weekend 2026
events.description: Gilroy Gardens Family Theme Park celebrates the Fourth of July with the Cherry Red, White & Blue Celebration, July 3-5, 2026, featuring extended hours from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. Families can enjoy gentle rides, water play areas, a backyard BBQ, and the final weekend of the Cherry Jubilee. A wonderful all-ages July 4th outing in the South Bay.

events.title: Gilroy Gardens Cherry Red, White & Blue July 4th Weekend 2026
events.description: Cap off the Fourth of July weekend at Gilroy Gardens Family Theme Park with the Cherry Red, White & Blue Celebration, July 3-5, 2026, featuring extended hours from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. Families can enjoy gentle rides, water play areas, a backyard BBQ, and the final day of the beloved Cherry Jubilee. A wonderful all-ages summer outing in the South Bay.
